etymology.description: semantic 人(人) (rén, "person") + phonetic 孚(孚) (fú, "to capture") -> captive
etymology.interpretation: The character 俘 (fú, "captive") combines 人(人) (rén, "person") indicating the human aspect, with 孚(孚) (fú, "to capture") as the phonetic component. This composition suggests a person who is captured or taken prisoner.

examples.chinese: 敌人士兵被俘(敵人士兵被俘)。
他们将俘虏送到营地(他們將俘虜送到營地)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen jiāng fúlǔ sòng dào yíngdì.
examples.english: They sent the captives to the camp.

examples.chinese: 在战斗中，许多俘虏被释放(在戰鬥中，許多俘虜被釋放)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhàndòu zhōng, xǔduō fúlǔ bèi shìfàng.
examples.english: During the battle, many captives were released.
